title: vicia sativa subsp. heterophylla
intro: Vicia sativa subsp. heterophylla, also known as winter vetch or broad-leaved vetch, is a subspecies of the vetch plant that belongs to the pea family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Vicia sativa subsp. heterophylla include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to alleviate respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
